blocks.content: Larry crossed the cafeteria toward the windows.
Maya was sitting with one other girl. She slid her bag off the seat across from her before he even asked.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You're not going to let that go.
Her friend — she said her name was Keisha, said it like Larry should already know it — raised an eyebrow at Maya and then went back to her food.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You're the one who came with Michaels this morning.
She said it in a way that meant she was watching him figure out what she'd actually meant. He ate his lunch.
Across the cafeteria, Jonathan was holding court at the center table. At some point he looked up and scanned the room. His eyes found Larry, registered him, moved on.
No nod. No wave. Just a read and a release, like checking a score.
